label: macroscopy
definition: Methods for imaging objects large enough to be observed by the unaided eye

label: recorded image
definition: Methods and devices used for capturing an image of a real object; distinct from images created by artwork

label: portrayed image
definition: Methods and devices used for creating images by some form of artwork, as distinct from capturing images by means of some recording device

label: animation
definition: A form of graphical illustration that changes with time to give a sense of motion or represent dynamic changes in the portrayal.

label: scanning probe microscopy
definition: Imaging by means of a physical probe that passes over the sample

label: FLIP
definition: Fluorescence loss in photobleaching; a method of determining whether two populations of fluorophore are in diffusive communication with one another by observing the decrease in brightness of one population when the other population is subjected to continuous photobleaching.

label: secondary_electron imaging
definition: Image created using the electrons generated by ionization of a sample due to inelastic scattering of the primary beam of radiation

label: back-scattered_electron imaging
definition: Imaged formed using electrons undergoing elastic scattering at very high angles, with emission of characteristic X-rays

label: slit-scan confocal microscopy
definition: Illumination and detection via a thin slit

label: array-scan confocal microscopy
definition: Fixed array of sources focused to an array of spots that is swept over the specimen in raster fashion

label: fluorescence polarization microscopy
definition: A mode of microscopy that detects the rotational motion of fluorophores.
